strengths: Two clearly distinct structural parameters (curveSoftness, structureComplexity) satisfy the acceptance criteria
All mappings produce normalized numbers — no raw browser strings can leak
Session stability is correctly ensured via pass-through in evolveParams (no drift)
Graceful null/zero fallbacks for every signal input
Test steps cover null handling, stability, boundary values, and rendering behavior

issues.description: types.ts is listed in files_to_modify but no step actually modifies it. VisualParams lives in mappings.ts. Remove types.ts from files_to_modify to avoid confusion.

profileToComplexity reuses DPR and hardwareConcurrency which already drive the existing `density` parameter via capabilityToDensity (lines 119-133). When step 6 then multiplies density by structureComplexity to compute effective particle count, the DPR/cores signal is double-counted — high-capability devices get compounding boosts, low-capability devices get compounding penalties. Fix: either (a) remove DPR/cores from profileToComplexity and use only screen aspect ratio + one other independent signal (e.g. prefersColorScheme or language-script complexity), or (b) keep the inputs but make step 6 use structureComplexity independently of density (e.g. scale size variance and hue spread only, not particle count).

Line references '95-100 for ctx.fillRect' are inaccurate. The actual draw function spans lines 49-102, and rendering logic (fillStyle, positioning, fillRect) is around lines 75-93. Update line references to match the actual code so the implementer doesn't waste time searching.

A hard threshold at 0.5 for shape switching (arc vs fillRect) creates a binary visual jump rather than a structural gradient. Consider a softer approach: use roundRect with a corner radius proportional to curveSoftness (0 = sharp rect, size/2 = full circle). This also avoids the branching concern mentioned in the step notes, since the same code path handles all values.

Scaling particle count in init() means structureComplexity is baked at initialization time. If the geometry system is ever re-initialized mid-session (e.g. on resize), the count stays correct, but this coupling between init-time params and draw-time params should be documented with a brief comment.

risk_notes: The main risk is the DPR/cores double-counting in density × structureComplexity. If left unfixed, low-end devices will render ~36% of base particles (0.6 density × 0.6 complexity factor) while high-end devices render 100%, creating a wider capability gap than intended. The rest of the plan is solid and well-structured.

failures.description: types.ts is listed in files_to_modify but no step actually modifies it. VisualParams lives in mappings.ts. Remove types.ts from files_to_modify to avoid confusion.

profileToComplexity reuses DPR and hardwareConcurrency which already drive the existing `density` parameter via capabilityToDensity (lines 119-133). When step 6 then multiplies density by structureComplexity to compute effective particle count, the DPR/cores signal is double-counted — high-capability devices get compounding boosts, low-capability devices get compounding penalties. Fix: either (a) remove DPR/cores from profileToComplexity and use only screen aspect ratio + one other independent signal (e.g. prefersColorScheme or language-script complexity), or (b) keep the inputs but make step 6 use structureComplexity independently of density (e.g. scale size variance and hue spread only, not particle count).

Line references '95-100 for ctx.fillRect' are inaccurate. The actual draw function spans lines 49-102, and rendering logic (fillStyle, positioning, fillRect) is around lines 75-93. Update line references to match the actual code so the implementer doesn't waste time searching.

A hard threshold at 0.5 for shape switching (arc vs fillRect) creates a binary visual jump rather than a structural gradient. Consider a softer approach: use roundRect with a corner radius proportional to curveSoftness (0 = sharp rect, size/2 = full circle). This also avoids the branching concern mentioned in the step notes, since the same code path handles all values.

Scaling particle count in init() means structureComplexity is baked at initialization time. If the geometry system is ever re-initialized mid-session (e.g. on resize), the count stays correct, but this coupling between init-time params and draw-time params should be documented with a brief comment.
